title: Find the Subtasks That Did Not Execute
description: Given Tasks with subtasks_count and Executed with completed subtasks, find (task_id, subtask_id) not executed.
constraints: task_id is the primary key of Tasks
(task_id, subtask_id) is the primary key of Executed
subtasks_count >= 1 — subtask IDs run from 1 to subtasks_count
Executed only contains subtasks that actually ran — missing ones are the answer
key_concept: Recursive CTE to generate all expected subtask numbers (1..N), then anti-join with executed.

hints.L1: First generate all expected (task_id, subtask_id) pairs. Then find which aren't in Executed.
hints.L2: Recursive CTE: anchor = (task_id, subtasks_count). Recursive: decrement subtask_id until 1.
hints.L3: WITH RECURSIVE gen AS (SELECT task_id, subtasks_count AS subtask_id FROM Tasks UNION ALL SELECT task_id, subtask_id-1 FROM gen WHERE subtask_id>1) SELECT g.task_id, g.subtask_id FROM gen g LEFT JOIN Executed e ON g.task_id=e.task_id AND g.subtask_id=e.subtask_id WHERE e.subtask_id IS NULL

optimal_solution:
WITH RECURSIVE gen AS (
  SELECT task_id, subtasks_count AS subtask_id FROM Tasks
  UNION ALL
  SELECT task_id, subtask_id - 1 FROM gen WHERE subtask_id > 1
)
SELECT g.task_id, g.subtask_id FROM gen g
LEFT JOIN Executed e ON g.task_id = e.task_id AND g.subtask_id = e.subtask_id
WHERE e.subtask_id IS NULL;
